assessments.rationale: Wind parks own MV collector + park substation + DSO/TSO interconnection. Co-located BESS taps existing MV infrastructure; curtailment implies headroom for charge/discharge.
9 MWh ≈ 720 m² at the 80 m²/MWh bound — trivial against wind park footprints of 0.5–1.5 ha per turbine. Adjacent-to-substation siting is standard.
Rural park siting provides ample separation to buildings and property lines; VdS clearance and Feuerwehr access easily met even for >3 MWh clusters.
Hazard is market-agnostic and contained at the product level (NOVEC 1230, compartmentalization, deflagration venting). Rural siting further reduces third-party exposure.

Base VDE-AR-N 4110 + FGW TR8 Type B is native capability. Incremental add: grid-forming firmware + FGW TR3 grid-forming cert to monetize inertia-service revenue. Capability extension, not a delivery barrier. Mitigation is value-accretive (unlocks revenue) rather than a pure cost.
assessments.mitigation: Select PCS SKU with grid-forming firmware option (SMA / Sungrow roadmap), Commission FGW TR3 grid-forming unit certification with TÜV or DEKRA, Register asset for BNetzA inertia-service market prequal (launching Jan 2026)
assessments.mitigationTime: parallel to standard TR8 dossier — no critical-path impact

assessments.rationale: Market-agnostic supply-chain exposure; not differentiating for 221115 vs. any other destination.
German onshore wind sites (coastal and inland) sit within −20 to +50 °C; container HVAC manages internal setpoint. Offshore / extreme-climate exposure is scoped out.
Market-agnostic thermodynamic floor; revenue-stack math at this market already assumes 10–15% loss budget and still yields ~€400k/MW/yr.

Wind+BESS co-location carries the strongest €/MW revenue stack (~€400k/MW/yr: curtailment capture + arbitrage + ancillary + inertia). Payback at €250–400/kWh installed lands in the 3–5 year band with margin. Cost can never be a knockout per component rules.
